hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
523   -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :   «
ق َّن َّص ْي ُّمْ وَّلَّذَّ ق ُّلاَّن ٍ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّون َّ ن َّعَّمْ رَّن  َّن َّا ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ ق َّمَّا ذ َّإ ق َّالَّ عَّن ْذِى َّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ُّ ق َّي َّق ُّول ون َّ جَّمِذَّك َّ رَّن  َّن َّا وَّ  إِسْي َّزْج َّغَّ 
ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ إِن ْن ُّوإ لَّهُّ ن َّي ْي اً ق ِى  إَّلْح َّن  َّه ِ ' وَّ سَّم ُّوةُّ ن َّي ْث َّ إَّلْحَّمْذِ .
hadiths.en:
it was narrated from Mehran ibn Muhammad that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"When a person dies, Allah {SWT}, the Exalted and Glorious, sends an angel to the one 
among their family who is most deeply affected by the loss. The angel touches their 
heart and alleviates the intensity of grief. If it were not for this, the world would not be 
able to continue”. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 227 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 228 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 
176 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 559 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 278 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 56, p. 188 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 522

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar: 524 - وَّ لَّم َّا مَّاب َّ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ ج َّزَّح إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّي َّق َّذ َّمَّ إَّلس َّزِي زَّ ن ِلاَّ جِذ َّإءٍ وَّ لأَّ رِذَّإءٍ .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said: "When the child of a believer passes away, 
Allah {SWT} – knowing best what His servant has said – asks the angels: 'Have you taken 
the child of My believing servant?' They reply: 'Yes, our Lord {AZJ}.'  
Allah {SWT} then asks: 'What did My servant say?'  
They say: "He praised You and said: "Indeed, we belong to Allah {SWT} and to Him we will 
return"  
Then Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, says: "'Build for My servant a house in Paradise and name 
it the House of Praise (Bayt al-Hamd).'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 523

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.en:
When Ismail ibn Jaafar passed away, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
came out and walked ahead of the bier without shoes and without an outer garment 
(ridā').  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 524

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
526   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ  ن َّق ُّولُّ مَّا  ي ُّسْح ِظُّ إَّلز َّب .
hadiths.en:
When Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain {a.s}, saw a funeral procession, He would say: 
'Praise be to Allah {SWT} who did not make me from among those whose lives were 
abruptly taken'". 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 167 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 177 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 1, p. 452 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 391 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 157 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 18, p. 155 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 525
reference.chapter: THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
527  - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ 
ر َّي ْذِ ن ْن ِ جَّارِي َّه َّ كَّان َّ إ ِذ َّإ ذَّج َّلَّ ن َّي ْي َّهُّ كَّي ُّزَّ ن ُّكَّاو ُّةُّ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا ج ِذ إً وَّ ي َّق ُّول ي ُّحَّذِّي َّان ِى  وَّ ي ُّو َّإن ِسَّان ِى  ق َّذ َّهَّن َّا ج َّمِي عاً .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"When Ibrahim, the son of the Messenger of Allah {saws} passed away, the Prophet said:  
'We are grieved by your departure, O’ Ibrahim, and we are patient. The heart is 
sorrowful, and the eyes shed tears, but we do not say that which displeases the Lord 
{AZJ}.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 177 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 571 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 280 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 4, p. 283 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 10, p. 398 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 526

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"When The Prophet {saws} received news of the deaths of Ja'far ibn Abi Talib {a.s} and Zayd 
ibn Harithah, he would weep intensely when he entered his house and would say,  
'They used to converse with me and bring me comfort, and now they are both gone.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 177 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 571 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 280 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 21, p. 55

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 527

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
529 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْكَّاهِلِى ِّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِن  َّ إِمْ زَّإ َّي ِى  وَّ إ ُّج ْي ِى  وَّ هِى َّ  
إِمْزَّإ َّة ُّ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مَّارِذٍ ي َّح ْزُّج َّان ِ ق ِى  إَّلْمَّا ي ِمِ ق َّا َّن ْهَّاهُّمَّا ق َّق َّالَّي َّا لِى  إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ  جَّزَّإماً إِن ْي َّهَّي ْن َّا عَّن ْهُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ جَّزَّإماً ق َّلِمَّ 
ي َّمْن َّعُّن َّا ق َّي َّمْي َّن ِغَّ إَّلن  َّاسُّ مِن ْ ق َّص َّاءِ جُّق ُّوق ِن َّا ق َّق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ن ِى  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّن ْعَّث ُّ إ ُّمِّى  
وَّ إ ُّم َّ ق َّزْوَّة َّ  ي َّق ْص ِي َّان ِ جُّق ُّوق َّ إ َّهْلِ إَّلْمَّذِي ن َّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, trials and patience race toward the believer, so when trials reach him, he is 
patient. But panic and trials race toward the disbeliever, so when trials reach him, he 
panics." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Muʾmin, Part 1, p. 63 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 223 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 
177 • Muskkin al-Fu’ād, Part 1, p. 51 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 566 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 256 • Bihar Al Anwar, 
Vol. 68, p. 95 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 142 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 65 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 528

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
530  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إَّلْن َّاق ُّون َّ مَّلْهُّو   عَّن ْهُّمْ إ ِلَّى ي َّوْمِ إَّلْق ِي َّامَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Al-Kahili that He said: "I said to Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa 
ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s}:  
‘My wife and my sister, who is the wife of Muhammad ibn Marid, go out to mourning 
gatherings. I prohibited them, but they said to me, 'If it is forbidden, we will refrain, but 
if it is not forbidden, why do you prevent us when people refrain from fulfilling our rights 
(if we do not attend)?’  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'You ask me about rights? My father {a.s} used to send my mother and 
Umm Farwah to fulfil the rights of the people of Medina.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 529
reference.chapter: THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٦، ح ٥٢٩

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"No one will be questioned in the grave except one who has perfected their faith 
completely or one who has perfected disbelief completely. As for the rest, they will be 
left alone until the Day of Resurrection."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 530
reference.chapter: THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
532  - وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ : «
إِي  َّح َّذ ُّوإ ق ُّن ُّورَّ إ َّن ْن ِي َّان ِهِمْ مَّسَّاج ِذ .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about 
visiting graves and building mosques around them.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "There is no issue with visiting graves, but no mosques should be 
built around them." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 228 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 178 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 578 
• Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 20 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 531

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
533   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ ج َّز َّإح  إَّلْمَّذَّإن ِن ِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  كَّي ْف َّ إَّلي  َّسْلِي مُّ عَّلَّى إ َّهْلِ إَّلْق ُّن ُّورِ ق َّق َّال 
إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّلَّى إ َّهْلِ إَّلذِّي َّارِ مِن َّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ وَّ إَّلْمُّسْلِمِي ن َّ رَّجِمَّ إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلْمُّسْي َّق ْذِمِي ن َّ مِن  َّا وَّ إَّ لْمُّسْي َّا ْج ِزِي ن َّ وَّ إ ِن  َّا إ ِن ْ س َّاءَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ن ِكُّمْ 
لأَّجِق ُّون َّ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet Muhammad {saws} said:  
"Do not take my grave as a qibla (direction of prayer) or a mosque. Indeed, Allah {SWT}, 
the Exalted and Glorious, cursed the Jews when they took the graves of their prophets 
as places of worship."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 178 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 451 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 
235 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 161 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 20 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 532

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.en:
Jarrah al-Mada'ini asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about how to 
offer salutations to the inhabitants of the graves. 
Imam {a.s} replied: "[You should stand and] say:  
'Peace be upon the inhabitants of the dwellings (graves) from among the believers and 
the Muslims. May Allah {SWT} have mercy on those of us who have gone ahead and those 
who will come later, and indeed, if Allah {SWT} wills, we shall join you.'" 
[REFERENCES] Kamil al-Ziyarat, Part 1, p. 322 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 179 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol. 99, p. 298 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 366

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 533

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
535   -  وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ لَّم َّا ذَّج َّلَّ إَّلْمَّق َّان ِز إ َّم َّا إَّلذ ُّورُّ ق َّق َّذْ سُّكِن َّث ْ وَّ 
إ َّم َّا إَّلْأ َّر ْوَّإح ُّ ق َّق َّذْ ن ُّكِحَّث ْ وَّ إ َّم َّا إَّلْأ َّمْوَّإلُّ ق َّق َّذْ ق ُّسِمَّث ْ، ق َّهَّذ َّإ ج َّن َّزُّ مَّ ا عِن ْذَّن َّا وَّ لَّي ْث َّ س ِعْزِى  مَّا عِن ْذَّكُّمْ 
إ َّصْحَّان ِهِ وَّق َّالَّ  «   »
hadiths.en:
When the Messenger of Allah {saws} would pass by graves, he would say:  
'Peace be upon you, O’ dwellers of the homes of believers. Indeed, if Allah {SWT} wills, 
we shall join you.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 179 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 581 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 534

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
536  -  وَّ وَّق َّف َّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّلَّى إَّلْق َّي ْلَّى ن ِن َّذْرٍ وَّ ق َّذْ ج َّمَّعَّهُّمْ ق ِى  ق َّلِي ث ٍ ق َّق َّال « 
إ ِن  َّا  »
ق َّن َّط َّزَّ إ ِلَّي ْهِمْ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} entered the 
graveyard, He said:  
"O’ inhabitants of the earth and estranged ones! As for your homes, they have been 
inhabited; As for your spouses, they have remarried; And as for your wealth, it has 
been divided. This is the news from our side ; if only I knew what news you have."  
Then he turned to his companions and said:  
"If they were permitted to respond, they would say, 'Indeed, the best provision is taqwā 
(God-consciousness).'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 180 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 581 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 535

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} stood over the bodies of those killed at Badr, 
having gathered them into a well/pit, and said: 
“O’ inhabitants of the well, we have found what our Lord {AZJ} promised us to be true; 
have you found what your Lord {AZJ} promised to be true?” 
The hypocrites replied: "The Messenger of Allah {saws} is speaking to the dead?"  
He then turned to them and said: "If they were permitted to speak, they would say, 
'Indeed, the best provision is taqwā (God-consciousness).'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 180 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 581

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 536

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.en:
Sayyida Fatimah Al -Zahra {S.A} used to visit the graves of the martyrs every 
Saturday morning, and She would come to the grave of Hamzah {a.s}, show mercy upon 
him, and seek forgiveness for him.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 180 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 465 • Ta ʿziyat al-
Ḥazānā, Part 1, p. 273 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 579 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 224 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 43, p. 
90 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 99, p. 300 • Awalim al-Ulum, Part 11, p. 269 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 537

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
539  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «
إ ِلَّى ذ َّلِك َّ وَّ مَّن ْ كَّان َّ مُّن َّاق ِق اً وَّج َّذَّ إ َّلَّمَّه .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "When you enter the graveyard, 
say : 
“Peace be upon the people of Paradise." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 180 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 582 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 226 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 538

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
540  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلْمَّوْي َّى ن َّز ُّورُّهُّمْ ق َّق َّال عَّمْ 
ق َّي َّعْلَّمُّون َّ ن ِن َّا إ ِذ َّإ إ َّي َّي ْن َّاهُّمْ ق َّق َّال   وَّ ي َّسْي َّا ْن ِسُّون َّ إ ِلَّي ْكُّمْ 
س َّى ْءٍ ن َّق ُّولُّ إ ِذ َّإ إ َّي َّي ْن َّاهُّمْ ق َّال  وَّ لَّق ِّهِمْ مِن ْك َّ رِص ْوَّإن اً وَّ 
إ َّسْكِن ْ إ ِلَّي ْهِمْ مِن ْ رَّجْمَّي ِك َّ مَّا ي َّ صِلُّ ن ِهِ وَّجْذَّي َّهُّمْ وَّ ي ُّو ْن ِسُّ ن ِهِ وَّجْس َّي َّهُّمْ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} said:  
"When you enter the graveyards, step over the graves. Whoever is a believer will find 
comfort from that, and whoever is a hypocrite will feel its pain."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 180 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 582 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 231 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 43 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 19 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 99, p. 300 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 539

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
541  -  وَّق َّال إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «۝  إ ِن ّٰا إ َّن ْز َّلْن ٰاةُّ ق ِى  لَّي ْلَّه ِ إَّلْق َّذْرِ   ۝»  
سَّن ْغَّ مَّز َّإب ٍ إ ِلأ َّ ع َّق َّزَّ إَّلل َّهُّ لَّهُّ وَّ لِصَّاجِث ِ إَّلْق َّن ْزِ .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim that He said: "I asked Imam Jafar 
ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}: Do the dead know when we visit them?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “Yes, by Allah {SWT}, they know of your visits and they rejoice and find 
comfort in your presence”  
I said: "What should we say when we visit them?" 
Imam {a.s} said: Say; 
"O’ Allah {SWT}, make the earth light upon their sides, raise their souls to You, grant them 
Your pleasure, and send upon them from Your mercy that which connects their 
loneliness and soothes their desolation. Truly, You are powerful over all things."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 180 • Falah Al -Sael, Part 1, p. 85 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 540

hadiths.chapter: 26. THE CHAPTER OF CONDOLENCES AND GRIEF DURING CALAMITIES, VISITING GRAVES, LAMENTATION AND
hadiths.ar:
542  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إ ِسْحَّاق ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلْأ َّو َّلَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِ ي َّز ُّورُّ إ َّهْلَّهُّ ق َّق َّال 
ق َّق َّال ن ْهُّمْ مَّن ْ ي َّز ُّورُّ ق ِى  كُّلِّ  
ي َّلاَّي َّه ِ إ َّي  َّامٍ رَّإ َّي ْث ُّ ق ِى  مَّح ْزَّى كَّلاَّمِهِ إ َّن  َّهُّ ي َّق ُّول 
إَّلس  َّمْسِ إ َّوْ ق ُّن َّي ْلَّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّي َّن ْعَّث ُّ   إَّلل َّهُّ مَّعَّهُّ مَّلَّكاً ي ُّزِي هِ مَّا ي َّسُّز ُّ ن ِهِ وَّ ي َّسْي ُّزُّ عَّن ْهُّ مَّا ي َّكْزَّهُّهُّ ق َّي َّزَّى سُّزُّورإً وَّ ي َّزْج ِغُّ إ ِلَّى ق ُّز َّة ِ  
عَّي ْن ٍ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s} said:  
"Whenever a believing servant visits the grave of a believer and recites 'Indeed, We 
revealed it [the Qur'an] on the Night of Qadr' Surah Al-Qadr (97:1-5) seven times, Allah 
{SWT} will forgive him and the person buried in the grave”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 181 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 577 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 227 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 5, p. 614 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 14, p. 355  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 26, H. 541
